---
title: "codon_within_condition_binomial — Olive report"
subtitle: "ribostall global_occupancy — occupancy stats"
date: 2026-06-06
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    embed-resources: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <style>
        table td, table th { padding-top: 0.5em; padding-bottom: 0.5em; }
        </style>
  pdf:
    toc: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{needspace}
        \renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.5}
tbl-colwidths: [8, 5, 5, 17, 14, 14, 37]
execute:
  echo: false
---

## Composite figure

![BWM 3x3 grid of unweighted codon binomial volcano panels: rows are the three BWM timepoints (top to bottom: Day 0, Day 5, Day 10); columns are ribosome sites in A / P / E order (left to right). Each panel plots -log10(FDR) on the y-axis against Log2 Enrichment on the x-axis (x range ~-2.5 to +3.5); each glyph is one of the 62 codons (61 sense + the in-frame stop TGA) labelled by its three-letter code and coloured by the amino-acid property of its translation (red = acidic, blue = basic, green = hydrophobic, orange = neutral, purple = polar, grey = stop). Triangles mark FDR<0.05 cells and circles mark non-significant cells; the dotted vertical line is Log2 Enrichment = 0, the dashed vertical lines mark +/-0.5, and the dashed horizontal line marks FDR = 0.05.](../plots/within_condition/codon/composite/unweighted/BWM_volcano_grid.png){fig-pos="H" width=92%}

![control 3x3 grid of unweighted codon binomial volcano panels: rows are the three control timepoints (top to bottom: Day 0, Day 5, Day 10); columns are ribosome sites in A / P / E order. Axes, glyph labelling, colour key, and dashed reference lines are identical to the BWM grid above.](../plots/within_condition/codon/composite/unweighted/control_volcano_grid.png){fig-pos="H" width=92%}

Each glyph is one codon tested against its transcriptome background frequency (`bg_freq`) in that group; a panel is one (group, site) BH-FDR family of 62 codons. A glyph to the right of zero is occupied more often than its transcriptomic abundance predicts in that group, to the left less often, and higher up the panel means a smaller FDR. The test is **within-group**: BWM and control are never compared directly here — that contrast lives in the per-timepoint Fisher sister file. Panels are arranged in A / P / E ribosome-site order across columns.

---

## Overview

- **CSV:** `codon_within_condition_binomial.csv`
- **Family (from `_INDEX.md`):** `within_condition_binomial`
- **Sister CSV(s):**
  - `aa_within_condition_binomial.csv`
- **Test:** One-sample binomial test (`observed_count` out of `total_n` vs H0: `observed_freq` = `bg_freq`, two-sided); BH-FDR within each (group, site) family of 62 codons (61 sense + in-frame stop TGA)
- **Design:** Per (group, site, codon) one-sample binomial: the observed `observed_count` is tested against the binomial null `Binomial(total_n, bg_freq)`, where `bg_freq` is the codon frequency in the transcriptome composition. 6 groups (BWM and control x d0/d5/d10) x 3 sites (A/P/E) x 62 codons = 1116 tests; replicates are pooled before the test (pseudoreplicated); BH-FDR is applied independently within each of 18 (group, site) families of 62 codons. The test is **within-group**: each group is tested against its own transcriptome background, never against another group.

### Family-level caveats

- **pseudorep** — replicates within a group are summed before `binomtest`. Standard pseudoreplication; anti-conservative.
- **iid-violation-binomial** — the binomial test assumes `total_n` iid Bernoulli draws with probability p_bg; ribosome footprints are not iid (one transcript contributes overlapping A/P/E windows, and transcripts vary widely in coverage). Headline-grade tiny p-values (down to ~1e-132) reflect this violation more than a per-feature biological signal. `log2_enrichment` is the primary effect column, not p.
- **bh-per-(group,site)** — BH-FDR is per (group,site) family of ~20 (aa) or ~61 (codon) tests. `p_adj` means "corrected within this group at this site", not across the whole CSV.
- **bg-transcriptome-freq** — the null frequency is the transcriptome codon/AA composition (the `Transcriptome` column), so enrichment means "more occupied than its transcriptomic abundance predicts", not vs a uniform expectation.

---

## Headline

Codon-level within-group binomial against `bg_freq`, 6 groups (BWM and control x d0/d5/d10) x 3 sites (A/P/E) x 62 codons (61 sense + the in-frame stop TGA) = 1116 tests; BH-FDR per (group, site) family of 62. 1092/1116 hits at `p_adj` < 0.05 (1095/1116 at `p_adj` < 0.10). File min `p_adj` underflows to exactly 0.0: 624 of 1116 cells return `p_adj` = 0.0 and the smallest nonzero `p_adj` is 5.93e-323 (BWM_day_0, P:CAG, `log2_enrichment` = -0.345, `observed_count` = 23483, `total_n` = 2110390). At whole-transcriptome `total_n` (~2.1M) every one of the 186 (site, codon) cells clears the `#sig` >= 2 floor, so significance separates nothing and ranking is by magnitude and reproducibility. The dominant structure is **cross-group concordance**: 55 of the 186 cells are concordantly enriched, 102 concordantly depleted, and 29 discordant across all 6 groups. The in-frame stop **TGA carries the largest `log2_enrichment` in the file** (site A/P/E means +2.68 / +2.18 / +2.03, individual values up to +3.53) but rests on 16-20 counts, so it carries `stop-codon-instability` and `rare-codon` - read its direction, not its magnitude. Among sense codons the largest-magnitude cells hold one sign across all 6 groups: E:ATA -1.49, P:ATA -1.46, P:CTA -1.26, E:AAG +1.22 (Lys), E:CTA -1.21, P:TTA -1.14, P:GGG -1.07. `p_adj` magnitudes are co-amplified by `iid-violation-binomial`; magnitude-plus-reproducibility is the anchor for reading.

## Top hits

The effect column is `log2_enrichment` = log2(observed `observed_freq` / `bg_freq`); positive means the codon is over-represented at that site in that group relative to its transcriptome abundance, negative under-represented. Top hits are three **cross-group** sub-tables that partition the 186 (site, codon) cells by the sign of `log2_enrichment` across the 6 groups (BWM and control x d0/d5/d10): concordant enrichment (positive in all 6), concordant depletion (negative in all 6), and discordant (>= 1 sign disagreement). A cell enters the partition only if it has a value in all 6 groups (all 186 do). Each sub-table shows every cell that reached `p_adj` < 0.05 in at least 2 of the 6 groups, a reproducibility floor on the `#sig` axis (`#sig` = groups with FDR<0.05, of 6), **not** a fixed row cap; here every cell clears it, so the three tables show all 55 / 102 / 29 cells. There is **no per-row p-value column**: significance is carried by the `#sig` count, and rows are sorted by site (A / P / E), then `#sig` descending, then `min count` (smallest `observed_count` across the 6 groups) descending. Ranking is deliberately not by p magnitude: `iid-violation-binomial` inflates p for modest effects at this `total_n` (624 cells underflow to `p_adj` = 0.0). In the `log2_enrichment` column the six values are listed on two lines — **BWM** day_0, day_5, day_10 on the first line, then **control** day_0, day_5, day_10 on the second. The `aa` column is the single-letter amino-acid translation of each codon (no parenthetical expansion); the codon cell stays bare. The `flag` column carries `iid-amp` (a group `p_adj` < 1e-10) and `rare-codon` (a group `observed_count` < 100; here only the three TGA cells); no codon's `bg_freq` exceeds 0.05, so `bg-tight` never fires.

```{=latex}
\needspace{22\baselineskip}
```

### Concordant enrichment — significant in >= 2 of 6 groups

| Site | codon | aa | log2_enrichment | min count | #sig | flag |

: Concordant enrichment — all 6 groups enriched; cells significant in >= 2 of 6 groups, sorted by site (A / P / E), then #sig then min count. {tbl-colwidths="[6,7,4,40,8,7,28]"}

```{=latex}
\clearpage
```

### Concordant depletion — significant in >= 2 of 6 groups

| Site | codon | aa | log2_enrichment | min count | #sig | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |

| A | GGG | G | -0.86, -1.04, -0.75 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -1.12, -1.20, -0.86 | 3155 | 6/6 | iid-amp |
| P | GAA | E | -0.26, -0.22, -0.14 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.22, -0.10, -0.12 | 48327 | 6/6 | iid-amp |
| P | AAA | K | -0.30, -0.26, -0.26 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.28, -0.23, -0.25 | 40232 | 6/6 | iid-amp |
| P | CAA | Q | -0.13, -0.13, -0.21 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.15, -0.34, -0.26 | 28508 | 6/6 | iid-amp |
| P | ATG | M | -0.33, -0.18, -0.23 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.20, -0.24, -0.27 | 25872 | 6/6 | iid-amp |
| P | ACT | T | -0.11, -0.07, -0.12 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.14, -0.10, -0.09 | 23043 | 6/6 | iid-amp |
| P | TTG | L | -0.35, -0.18, -0.22 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.20, -0.13, -0.31 | 20613 | 6/6 | iid-amp |
| P | TTT | F | -0.62, -0.53, -0.52 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.75, -0.33, -0.58 | 19575 | 6/6 | iid-amp |
| P | GCA | A | -0.54, -0.74, -0.56 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.71, -0.65, -0.53 | 17855 | 6/6 | iid-amp |
| P | TCT | S | -0.27, -0.21, -0.30 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.35, -0.25, -0.38 | 16106 | 6/6 | iid-amp |

: Concordant depletion — all 6 groups depleted; cells significant in >= 2 of 6 groups, sorted by site (A / P / E), then #sig then min count. {tbl-colwidths="[6,7,4,40,8,7,28]"}

```{=latex}
\clearpage
```

### Discordant — significant in >= 2 of 6 groups

| E | ATG | M | -0.12, -0.04, +0.01 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.07, +0.16, -0.02 | 30636 | 5/6 | iid-amp |
| E | GCT | A | +0.06, +0.02, +0.02 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.01, -0.20, -0.02 | 27992 | 5/6 | iid-amp |
| E | GTT | V | +0.13, +0.09, +0.04 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.03, -0.00, -0.01 | 30584 | 4/6 | iid-amp |

: Discordant — at least one sign disagreement across the 6 groups; cells significant in >= 2 of 6 groups, sorted by site (A / P / E), then #sig then min count. {tbl-colwidths="[6,7,4,40,8,7,28]"}

### Flag glossary

- `iid-amp` — short for "**i.i.d.-violation amplified**" (i.i.d. = independent and identically distributed; see the `iid-violation-binomial` caveat under Family-level caveats). Applied to every Top-hits row where at least one group has `p_adj` < 1e-10. The binomial test assumes every counted position is an independent coin-flip, but footprints on the same transcript move together — so the test acts as if it has far more independent evidence than it does, and even a modest enrichment gets an astronomically tiny p. The flag is a reminder that this row's p looks tiny because of the broken independence assumption, not because the effect is biologically large. Read it by `log2_enrichment` + reproducibility, never by how small the p is. Every displayed cell carries it here, so it does not separate rows; the `min count` column and the `rare-codon` flag do.
- `rare-codon` — at least one of the 6 groups has `observed_count` below the stability threshold (k < 100). The flag names the low-count groups — it lists them (e.g. `rare-codon (BWM d0, BWM d5, ctrl d10)`) and collapses to `(all)` when no group clears the threshold. Here it fires only on the three in-frame stop TGA cells (min counts 16-20); every sense codon has `min count` >= 2573 (E:AGG), so no sense codon is rare. Direction is reliable for a rare-codon row; magnitude is not.

## Numbers at a glance
- `n_tests`: 1116 (6 groups x 3 sites x 62 codons; 61 sense + in-frame stop TGA)
- `n_significant` (adjusted-p < 0.05): 1092
- `n_significant` (adjusted-p < 0.10): 1095
- `min adjusted-p`: 0.0 (underflow) - 624 of 1116 cells return `p_adj` exactly 0.0; smallest nonzero `p_adj` is 5.93e-323 (BWM_day_0, P:CAG; log2 -0.345, k=23483, n=2110390). Under `iid-violation-binomial` this is not a magnitude claim.
- `p_floor`: n/a - no exact-test floor for the binomial at these n.
- Per-(group, site) hits at `p_adj` < 0.05 (each is 62 codons; A / P / E order; counts sum to 1092):
  - BWM_day_0: A 59/62, P 62/62, E 62/62
  - BWM_day_5: A 59/62, P 61/62, E 61/62
  - BWM_day_10: A 59/62, P 62/62, E 59/62
  - control_day_0: A 61/62, P 62/62, E 59/62
  - control_day_5: A 62/62, P 62/62, E 59/62
  - control_day_10: A 62/62, P 60/62, E 61/62

## Biological interpretation

**The dominant signal of this file is cross-group concordance at codon resolution, not perturbation (concrete).** Of the 186 (site, codon) cells, 55 are concordantly enriched and 102 concordantly depleted across all 6 groups (BWM and control x d0/d5/d10), leaving 29 discordant — the partition feeding the three Top-hits tables. The largest-magnitude cells hold one direction across all 6 groups, and the discordant cells that do flip sit near zero. Because the test is within-group against each group's own transcriptome background, these counts are a structural map of which codons are over- and under-occupied at each ribosome site relative to their transcriptome abundance, stable across BWM perturbation and the d0/d5/d10 time axis. As in the sister amino-acid file, perturbation contrasts (BWM vs control, time-resolved) live in the per-timepoint Fisher and within-condition Fisher files; this file's biology is the baseline codon-occupancy shape.

**E-site lysine and the acidic codons are the strongest reproducible enrichments, matching the amino-acid sister at codon resolution (concrete).** E:AAG (Lys) is the single largest sense enrichment (+1.06 to +1.40 across the 6 groups, `min count` 72657), with E:AAA (Lys) co-enriched, and the acidic codons GAT/GAC (Asp) and GAG/GAA (Glu) concordantly enriched across all three sites (e.g. P:GAT +0.47 to +0.75, A:GAG/E:GAG). This is the codon-level image of the amino-acid sister's E:K and P:D / acidic-residue enrichment story. Mechanistically, E-site lysine is a textbook ribosome-dwell anchor (the positively charged side chain interacts electrostatically with the negatively charged exit tunnel after translocation), and acidic residues slow peptidyl-transfer chemistry; the codon resolution shows the lysine signal is carried by both AAG and AAA and the aspartate signal by both GAT and GAC.

**Within several amino acids the C-ending synonym is over-occupied while its A/G/T-ending sisters are depleted — the central codon-versus-amino-acid refinement (concrete).** Alanine is the clearest case: GCC is concordantly enriched at all three sites (A +0.05 to +0.53, P/E similar) while GCA and GCG are concordantly depleted at all three sites, so the amino-acid sister's uniform alanine depletion resolves at codon level into an enriched C-ending synonym plus depleted A/G-ending sisters. The same split runs through isoleucine (ATC enriched vs ATA/ATT depleted), valine (GTC enriched vs GTA/GTG depleted), threonine (ACC enriched vs ACA/ACG/ACT depleted), and the C-ending synonyms of histidine (CAC), asparagine (AAC), tyrosine (TAC), and phenylalanine (TTC) sitting in the enrichment set while their T-ending sisters deplete. A consistent split of this kind points to codon-level decoding (wobble-position / codon-optimality differences between synonyms) rather than an amino-acid side-chain property — the diagnostic question this codon file exists to answer, and the place it most clearly adds to the amino-acid sister.

**The largest-magnitude depletions are low-usage codons, and the small/short-side-chain residues are depleted as the structural counterpart to the K/D/E enrichment (concrete).** The most extreme sense depletions are rare A/T-rich codons — E:ATA (Ile) -1.49, P:ATA -1.46, P:CTA / E:CTA (Leu) ~-1.2, P:TTA (Leu) -1.14, P:GGG (Gly) -1.07, with CGG/AGG (Arg) and CCC (Pro) near -1 — each concordant across all 6 groups but resting on the smallest sense `min count` values (2573-5000), consistent with rarely-used codons being under-occupied because they are decoded and cleared without accumulating footprints. Layered on top, the small and hydroxyl-bearing residues (serine through TCA/TCT/TCG/AGT/AGC, threonine through ACA/ACG/ACT, alanine through GCA/GCG) are concordantly depleted at all three sites, the inverse of the charged-residue enrichment story by the same amino-acid-property logic. Read the rare-codon depletions by direction and `min count`, not by raw magnitude.

**The headline-grade p magnitudes (624 cells underflow to 0.0) are iid-violation amplification, not biological strength (concrete).** 624 of 1116 cells return `p_adj` exactly 0.0 and the smallest nonzero is 5.93e-323 (BWM_day_0 P:CAG, `log2_enrichment` only -0.345). At `total_n` ~2.1M the binomial p collapses toward zero for any non-trivial deviation, because the null assumes that many independent Bernoulli draws but ribosome footprints are not independent (one transcript contributes overlapping A/P/E windows and transcripts vary in coverage) — the `iid-violation-binomial` caveat. Every displayed cell therefore carries `iid-amp`, so the p column would separate nothing; read this file by `log2_enrichment` magnitude and cross-group reproducibility, with `min count` as the support check.

**The in-frame stop TGA and the discordant cells are exploratory, read by direction only (exploratory).** TGA carries the file's largest `log2_enrichment` (A/P/E means +2.68 / +2.18 / +2.03, up to +3.53 in a single group) and sits in the concordant-enrichment set, but it rests on only 16-20 counts and is mechanistically distinct from sense-codon occupancy, so its direction (consistently positive) is suggestive of readthrough/stop-context occupancy at most — not a magnitude to rank against high-count sense codons (`stop-codon-instability`, `rare-codon`). The 29 discordant cells (e.g. A:TGC, P:AAT, E:TTC) sit within roughly +/-0.3 and cluster near zero, more consistent with sign noise around a near-zero true effect than a reproducible group-specific switch. Worth checking whether TGA reaches a comparable extreme in any large-N Fisher file (a falsifier for the stop-instability reading) and whether any discordant cell flips group-consistently in the per-timepoint Fisher; absent that, treat both as not-a-hit. Exploratory only.

**What to carry forward.** *Concrete*: the cross-group concordance set (55 concordantly enriched + 102 concordantly depleted of the 186 cells) as a structural map of codon occupancy stable across perturbation and time; E:AAG/AAA and the acidic codons (GAT/GAC, GAG/GAA) as the strongest enriched cells; the C-ending-synonym-enriched / A-G-T-ending-depleted splits (Ala GCC, Ile ATC, Val GTC, Thr ACC, plus CAC/AAC/TAC/TTC) as the codon-resolution refinement of the amino-acid sister; rare-codon and small-residue depletions as the structural counterpart. *Exploratory*: the in-frame stop TGA (direction only, 16-20 counts) and the 29 discordant cells (near-zero, direction unreliable). *Noise*: any per-cell p-magnitude reading below the iid-amplification floor — quote magnitude + reproducibility + `min count`, not p. Cross-checks worth running in sister files: does the per-timepoint Fisher overlay BWM-vs-control divergence on any of these concordance cells (perturbation on top of the structural map); does the amino-acid sister's concordance set re-aggregate cleanly onto these codon-level splits.

## Methods

The test is a one-sample binomial (`observed_count` ~ Binomial(`total_n`, `bg_freq`), two-sided), with BH-FDR computed within each (group, site) family of 62 codon tests (each of the 18 sub-families is corrected independently, not against the full 1116). The 62 codons are the 61 sense codons plus the in-frame stop TGA (TAA and TAG are absent from this file). Effect column is `log2_enrichment` = log2(`observed_freq` / `bg_freq`); a count-weighted variant `weighted_log2_enrichment` is also present in the CSV but is not used for ranking here (the unweighted log2 is what the binomial p reflects). The null `bg_freq` is the transcriptome codon composition, so a positive `log2_enrichment` means "occupied more often than its transcriptomic abundance predicts," not "more occupied than control." The test answers "is codon X observed at this site at a different frequency than its transcriptome `bg_freq` in the same group?", **not** "is codon X enriched in BWM relative to control" (that is the per-timepoint Fisher) and **not** "is the occupancy shifting across timepoints" (that is between-timepoint Wilcoxon). Counts (`observed_count`) and totals (`total_n`) are summed across replicates before the test, hence the family-level pseudoreplication caveat. Top hits are presented as three cross-group tables that partition the 186 (site, codon) cells by the sign of `log2_enrichment` across the 6 groups: concordant enrichment (positive in all 6 groups; 55 cells), concordant depletion (negative in all 6; 102 cells), and discordant (at least one sign disagreement; 29 cells). A cell enters the partition only if it has a value in all 6 groups. Each table shows every cell that reached `p_adj` < 0.05 in at least 2 of the 6 groups (`#sig` >= 2, a reproducibility floor rather than a fixed row cap; all 186 cells clear it), the kept cells sorted by site (A / P / E), then `#sig` descending, then `min count` (smallest `observed_count` across the 6 groups) descending, with an `aa` column added per the codon-table convention. Ranking is by magnitude and reproducibility, not p, because at `total_n` ~2.1M the binomial p collapses toward zero (624/1116 underflow to exactly 0.0) for modest effects.

## What the caveats mean for reading this file

- **pseudorep.** The binomial pools replicates into one `observed_count` and one `total_n` per (group, site, codon) cell; per-replicate variation is invisible to the statistic, so the smallest p magnitudes are inflated, not additional certainty.
- **iid-violation-binomial.** The binomial null assumes the `total_n` counted positions are i.i.d. (independent and identically distributed), but footprints on the same transcript are correlated and transcripts vary in coverage; this is the dominant reason p-values compress toward zero (624/1116 to exactly 0.0). Rank by `log2_enrichment` + `observed_count` + cross-group reproducibility, not by p magnitude.
- **bh-per-(group, site).** BH-FDR is per (group, site) family of 62, so FDR control is within each 62-codon family, not pooled across the 186 codons per group or across the merged A/P/E file. Two rows with the same raw p in different (group, site) families can land at different `p_adj`.
- **bg-transcriptome-freq.** The null is the transcriptome codon composition, so "enrichment" means "occupied more than its transcriptomic abundance predicts," not vs a uniform expectation or vs control.
- **stop-codon-instability.** The in-frame stop TGA carries the file's largest `log2_enrichment` (A/P/E means +2.68 / +2.18 / +2.03, up to +3.53 in single groups) but rests on 16-20 counts; read its direction (consistently positive across all 6 groups), never its magnitude, and do not rank it alongside high-count sense codons.
- **rare-codon-low-count.** The `rare-codon` flag (any group `observed_count` < 100) fires only on the three TGA cells (min counts 16-20); every sense codon has `min count` >= 2573 (E:AGG), so no sense codon is low-count and the concern is confined to TGA, coinciding with `stop-codon-instability`.

### Considered and ruled out

*(none — both codon-specific caveats (`stop-codon-instability`, `rare-codon-low-count`) were confirmed, and the four family-wide caveats are inherited from `_INDEX.md`; no caveat was raised and ruled out for this file.)*

---

## Plots — individual

Each panel below is one (group, site) BH-FDR family of 62 codons, plotted as a volcano: `log2_enrichment` (x-range ~-2.5 to +3.5) on the x-axis, -log10(FDR) on the y-axis. Triangles mark FDR<0.05; circles are non-significant. Codons are labelled by three-letter code and coloured by the amino-acid property of their translation (red = acidic, blue = basic, green = hydrophobic, orange = neutral, purple = polar, grey = stop). The horizontal dashed line marks FDR = 0.05; the vertical dashed lines mark log2 = +/-0.5. At whole-transcriptome n almost every codon is pinned at the top of each panel (the FDR underflows), so each panel mainly resolves the x-axis spread; the in-frame stop TGA is the far-right enriched outlier in every panel.

![BWM Day 0, A site. Most codons pinned at the top (FDR underflows). The in-frame stop TGA is the far-right outlier (~+2.5, enriched). Among sense codons the largest enrichments are AAG (Lys, ~+1.0) and CGC (Arg, ~+0.95); the largest depletions are AGT/TTT (Ser/Phe, ~-0.9) and GGG (Gly, ~-0.86).](../plots/within_condition/codon/individual/unweighted/BWM_day_0_A_volcano.png)

![BWM Day 0, P site. ATA (Ile, ~-1.63) is the far-left depletion and the panel's largest-magnitude sense cell; TTA (Leu) and CTA (Leu) trail it on the depleted side. The largest sense enrichment is CGT (Arg, ~+0.88); the in-frame stop TGA sits to the right (~+1.4, enriched).](../plots/within_condition/codon/individual/unweighted/BWM_day_0_P_volcano.png)

![BWM Day 0, E site. ATA (Ile, ~-1.59) is the far-left depletion; CTA (Leu) and GGG (Gly) follow. AAG (Lys, ~+1.24) is the largest sense enrichment on the right; the in-frame stop TGA sits at ~+1.15 (enriched).](../plots/within_condition/codon/individual/unweighted/BWM_day_0_E_volcano.png)

![BWM Day 5, A site. The in-frame stop TGA is the far-right outlier (~+3.0, enriched). Largest sense enrichments CGC/AAG (Arg/Lys, ~+0.95); largest depletion GGG (Gly, ~-1.04), with CCC (Pro) and AGT (Ser) nearby.](../plots/within_condition/codon/individual/unweighted/BWM_day_5_A_volcano.png)

![BWM Day 5, P site. ATA (Ile, ~-1.38) is the far-left depletion; CTA (Leu) and GGG (Gly) follow. Largest sense enrichment CGT (Arg, ~+0.84); the in-frame stop TGA sits to the right (~+2.5, enriched).](../plots/within_condition/codon/individual/unweighted/BWM_day_5_P_volcano.png)

![BWM Day 5, E site. ATA (Ile, ~-1.42) is the far-left depletion; CTA (Leu) and GGG (Gly) follow. AAG (Lys, ~+1.13) is the largest sense enrichment; the in-frame stop TGA sits to the right (~+2.35, enriched).](../plots/within_condition/codon/individual/unweighted/BWM_day_5_E_volcano.png)

![BWM Day 10, A site. The in-frame stop TGA is the far-right outlier (~+3.5, the panel and file maximum, enriched). Largest sense enrichment CGC (Arg, ~+0.92); largest depletion CCC (Pro, ~-0.89), with CTA (Leu) and GGG (Gly) nearby.](../plots/within_condition/codon/individual/unweighted/BWM_day_10_A_volcano.png)

![BWM Day 10, P site. ATA (Ile, ~-1.36) is the far-left depletion; CTA (Leu) follows. GAT (Asp, ~+0.70) and CGT (Arg, ~+0.82) are the largest sense enrichments; the in-frame stop TGA sits to the right (~+3.14, enriched).](../plots/within_condition/codon/individual/unweighted/BWM_day_10_P_volcano.png)

![BWM Day 10, E site. ATA (Ile, ~-1.40) is the far-left depletion; CTA (Leu) follows. AAG (Lys, ~+1.06) is the largest sense enrichment; the in-frame stop TGA is the far-right outlier (~+3.30, enriched).](../plots/within_condition/codon/individual/unweighted/BWM_day_10_E_volcano.png)

![control Day 0, A site. The in-frame stop TGA is the far-right outlier (~+2.6, enriched). Largest sense enrichment AAG (Lys, ~+1.07); largest depletion GGG (Gly, ~-1.12), with CTA (Leu) and AGT (Ser) nearby. Densest A-site panel (61/62 hits).](../plots/within_condition/codon/individual/unweighted/control_day_0_A_volcano.png)

![control Day 0, P site. ATA (Ile, ~-1.57) is the far-left depletion; CTA (Leu) and CGG (Arg) follow. AAG (Lys, ~+0.97) and CGT (Arg, ~+0.89) are the largest sense enrichments; the in-frame stop TGA sits to the right (~+1.83, enriched). Full 62/62 P-site hits.](../plots/within_condition/codon/individual/unweighted/control_day_0_P_volcano.png)

![control Day 0, E site. ATA (Ile, ~-1.69) is the far-left depletion and the file's most extreme sense cell; CTA (Leu) and GGG (Gly) follow. AAG (Lys, ~+1.33) is the largest sense enrichment; the in-frame stop TGA sits at ~+1.77 (enriched).](../plots/within_condition/codon/individual/unweighted/control_day_0_E_volcano.png)

![control Day 5, A site. The in-frame stop TGA is the far-right outlier (~+3.1, enriched). Largest sense enrichment AAG (Lys, ~+1.05); largest depletion GGG (Gly, ~-1.20), with CCC (Pro) and CCT (Pro) nearby. Full 62/62 A-site hits.](../plots/within_condition/codon/individual/unweighted/control_day_5_A_volcano.png)

![control Day 5, P site. ATA (Ile, ~-1.31) is the far-left depletion; GGG (Gly) and CTA (Leu) follow. Largest sense enrichment CGT (Arg, ~+0.79); the in-frame stop TGA sits to the right (~+2.74, enriched). Full 62/62 P-site hits.](../plots/within_condition/codon/individual/unweighted/control_day_5_P_volcano.png)

![control Day 5, E site. AAG (Lys, ~+1.40) is the largest sense enrichment and the file maximum sense magnitude; ATA (Ile, ~-1.38) is the far-left depletion. The in-frame stop TGA sits to the right (~+2.47, enriched).](../plots/within_condition/codon/individual/unweighted/control_day_5_E_volcano.png)

![control Day 10, A site. The in-frame stop TGA is the far-right outlier (~+1.4, the smallest TGA value across the 6 groups, still enriched). Largest sense enrichment CGC (Arg, ~+1.01); largest depletion GGG (Gly, ~-0.86), with CTA (Leu) nearby. Full 62/62 A-site hits.](../plots/within_condition/codon/individual/unweighted/control_day_10_A_volcano.png)

![control Day 10, P site. ATA (Ile, ~-1.50) is the far-left depletion; TTA (Leu) and CTA (Leu) follow. GAT (Asp, ~+0.75) and CGT (Arg, ~+0.86) are the largest sense enrichments; the in-frame stop TGA sits to the right (~+1.46, enriched).](../plots/within_condition/codon/individual/unweighted/control_day_10_P_volcano.png)

![control Day 10, E site. AAG (Lys, ~+1.17) is the largest sense enrichment on the right; ATA (Ile, ~-1.46) is the far-left depletion, with CTA (Leu) and TTT (Phe) following. The in-frame stop TGA sits at ~+1.14 (enriched).](../plots/within_condition/codon/individual/unweighted/control_day_10_E_volcano.png)
